evals.prompt: Add a method to SampleService that fetches a list of items, with caching. Use Hoist's caching primitives.
evals.expected_output: The agent invokes the using-hoist-core-reference skill, looks up Cache or Timer in the reference tools, and proposes a method that uses them correctly.
expectations: The skill `using-hoist-core-reference` was invoked
The agent called either `mcp__hoist-core__hoist-core-search-symbols` or `mcp__hoist-core__hoist-core-get-members` (or the CLI equivalent if available) to look up Cache
The proposed code uses Hoist's `Cache` (or `CachedValue`/`Timer`) class, not a hand-rolled map

evals.prompt: Add a new endpoint to SampleController that returns paged results. Use Hoist conventions.
evals.expected_output: The agent invokes the skill, looks up BaseController's render helpers, and proposes an endpoint using `renderJSON` (or similar).
expectations: The skill `using-hoist-core-reference` was invoked
The agent consulted the reference tools for BaseController members or render conventions
The proposed endpoint uses Hoist's render helper (e.g. `renderJSON`) and follows the pattern in the existing methods

evals.prompt: How do I expose a Hoist config to SampleService so it reads a configurable refresh interval?
evals.expected_output: The agent invokes the skill, queries docs/symbols for ConfigService, and proposes the right injection and access pattern.
expectations: The skill `using-hoist-core-reference` was invoked
The agent looked up ConfigService (or `configService` injection) via the reference tools
The proposed code reads the config via Hoist's mechanism, not a hardcoded value or environment variable

evals.prompt: Add cluster-aware behavior to SampleService so cache invalidation broadcasts to other nodes.
evals.expected_output: The agent invokes the skill, looks up ClusterService or distributed cache patterns, and proposes the right approach.
expectations: The skill `using-hoist-core-reference` was invoked
The agent searched docs/symbols for cluster, ClusterService, or distributed cache
The proposed solution uses Hoist's cluster primitives (e.g. ClusterService, distributed Cache)

evals.prompt: I'm new to hoist-core. Show me where to start reading docs to understand BaseService, BaseController, and the common services.
evals.expected_output: The agent invokes the skill, queries the docs index, and surfaces the relevant README locations.
expectations: The skill `using-hoist-core-reference` was invoked
The agent called the docs index tool (`hoist-core-list-docs`, `hoist-core-search-docs` with broad query, or CLI equivalent if available)
The agent referenced specific hoist-core docs by id

evals.prompt: Add a /health endpoint to PlainSpringController that includes the JVM uptime in milliseconds. Don't introduce dependencies.
evals.expected_output: The agent does NOT invoke the using-hoist-core-reference skill. This is plain Spring with no hoist-core imports.
expectations: The skill `using-hoist-core-reference` was NOT invoked
The agent added the endpoint without consulting hoist-core reference tools

evals.prompt: Add a `dependencies` block stub to a fresh build.gradle for a Spring Boot project. No hoist-core needed.
evals.expected_output: The agent does NOT invoke the using-hoist-core-reference skill.
expectations: The skill `using-hoist-core-reference` was NOT invoked

evals.prompt: Refactor PlainSpringController so the health response key uses the constant 'STATUS_KEY' defined in a sibling Constants class.
evals.expected_output: The agent does NOT invoke the using-hoist-core-reference skill.
expectations: The skill `using-hoist-core-reference` was NOT invoked
The agent refactored without consulting hoist-core tools

evals.prompt: Set up the hoist-core MCP server and CLI tools in this Hoist app so AI agents can use them. We're on hoistCoreVersion 39.0.
evals.expected_output: The agent invokes the using-hoist-core-reference skill, follows the install procedure (Gradle snippet, installHoistCoreTools task, .mcp.json wiring with --source bundled launcher).
expectations: The skill `using-hoist-core-reference` was invoked
The agent proposed adding a `hoistCoreCli` configuration and `installHoistCoreTools` Sync task to `build.gradle`
The agent's mcp launcher passes `--source bundled` (or otherwise references the bundled content path) so the MCP server works without a hoist-core checkout sibling
The agent updated (or proposed updating) `.mcp.json` to point the `hoist-core` server at `./bin/hoist-core-mcp`

evals.prompt: Our app's hoistCoreVersion was just bumped to 39.0. Refresh the project-local hoist-core CLI launchers so they point at the new version.
evals.expected_output: The agent invokes the using-hoist-core-reference skill and re-runs the install task (or guides the user to) to refresh the version-locked launchers.
expectations: The skill `using-hoist-core-reference` was invoked
The agent's response involves re-running `./gradlew installHoistCoreTools` (or invoking it for the user) to regenerate launchers against the new JAR
The agent did NOT propose hand-editing the launcher scripts directly

evals.prompt: I want the hoist-core docs CLI installed in this app so I can search hoist-core docs from the shell without MCP. The app already depends on hoist-core 39.0.
evals.expected_output: The agent invokes the using-hoist-core-reference skill and walks through the install procedure (Gradle snippet + install task + bin launchers).
expectations: The skill `using-hoist-core-reference` was invoked
The agent's plan produces the `./bin/hoist-core-docs` launcher via the `installHoistCoreTools` Gradle task
The agent did NOT suggest a npm/npx-based installation path (which is hoist-react's model, not hoist-core's)

evals.prompt: Add a new direct dependency on `org.apache.commons:commons-lang3:3.14.0` to this Hoist app's build.gradle. Keep the existing dependencies untouched.
evals.expected_output: The agent does NOT invoke the using-hoist-core-reference skill — this is a routine Gradle dependency edit, not anything to do with the hoist-core MCP/CLI tools.
expectations: The skill `using-hoist-core-reference` was NOT invoked
The agent edited `build.gradle` directly without consulting hoist-core MCP/CLI install reference

evals.prompt: Bump grailsVersion in gradle.properties from 7.0.7 to 7.0.9. Don't touch anything else.
evals.expected_output: The agent does NOT invoke the using-hoist-core-reference skill — this is a routine version bump unrelated to hoist-core MCP/CLI install.
expectations: The skill `using-hoist-core-reference` was NOT invoked
